title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 9th December 2025 | TV5 Money Live

recommendations.note: Governance/accounting doubts; do not bottom-fish, exit if held
recommendations.detail: Broking houses raised accounting and corporate-governance doubts, prompting fund managers and long-term investors to cut positions. The stock fell sharply from around 7,500 to 3,600-3,700 (down 12% then a 4% technical bounce) and is range-bound roughly 3,500-4,500. Do not bottom-fish since long-term holders won't return quickly; if already holding, exit once the two-to-three-day confusion clears.

recommendations.note: Valuation too high (mkt cap ~40,000 cr); sell into any strength
recommendations.detail: Corrected about 30 rupees from the ~160 listing to around 135-136. Despite a quarterly update showing about 60% profit growth, its market cap of ~40,000 crore is very high and cannot be justified. If the stock reacts and looks slightly better, treat it as an opportunity to sell, recalling how education stocks behaved.

recommendations.note: Slowly accumulate; near 52-week high, strong Q result
recommendations.detail: One of the very few stocks near its 52-week high, with an excellent last-quarter result. Crop-protection chemical and fertilizer companies are slightly better after the trade crisis given good demand, and the Rabi season is ahead. Buy and slowly accumulate with a stop loss around 700.

recommendations.note: Nothing wrong; start accumulating on correction
recommendations.detail: Corrected from 19,000 to about 13,500 along with Kaynes and other EMS players. It is not a cheap stock, but it is a company that can generate huge volumes and revenue. Bottom-fish slowly and start accumulating.

recommendations.note: Slowly accumulate from 385; good contra bet
recommendations.detail: Focus is on bio-similar division absorption and how much the promoters pump in under QIP. The stock went to about 420 and has corrected to 385. From 385, slowly accumulate; it can be one of the outperformers and a good contra bet, though it is not a cheap stock.

recommendations.note: Frontline pick for ~Rs 1 lakh deployment; looks extremely good
recommendations.detail: For an investor with experience deploying about 1 lakh amid the current volatility and weakness, HUL is a frontline name that looks extremely good. He suggested either HUL or L&T for the amount.

recommendations.note: Frontline pick for ~Rs 1 lakh deployment; looks extremely good
recommendations.detail: For a ~Rs 1 lakh deployment amid current volatility, L&T is a frontline name that looks extremely good. He suggested picking either L&T or Hindustan Unilever.

recommendations.note: Strongest of the three; possible divestment; semi-defence transition
recommendations.detail: Of the caller's three holdings it shows the most strength. A possible minor government divestment before March-end, if it hits the news, could recover the stock closer to 2,000. It is slowly transforming into a semi-defence company, so there is no need to fear - hold.

recommendations.note: Heavy institutional selling but excellent turnaround; hold
recommendations.detail: It is consolidating between 50 and 60 with heavy institutional selling and corrected to around 50. However, company performance is extremely good and it showed a good turnaround result last time, so hold.

recommendations.note: Bought high; infra weakness weighing; fundamentals unchanged
recommendations.detail: The caller bought a bit high. The across-the-board weakness in infrastructure is weighing on NCC, but the fundamentals haven't changed - only the market condition is bad. Patience is needed; if held, the price will just come. Hold.

recommendations.note: Governance issues, flat results; exit into any Q3 fancy
recommendations.detail: Rose on hype and then corrected to near 52-week lows, with corporate-governance issues surfacing and extremely flat results, so no rally in six-to-seven months. The buyer's price is unlikely to come soon; maybe after the Q3 result if some fancy returns, exit technically around 130-140.

recommendations.note: PSU merger candidate; caller in 40% loss, exit near 45
recommendations.detail: A PSU stock the government is merging smaller banks into. The caller is in a 40% loss, meaning he bought very high (around a 50-60 rally). The last QIP price was around 42, so getting the entry price back is difficult; only when it comes around 45 should he exit.

recommendations.note: Fertilizer turnaround; buy with strict stop loss
recommendations.detail: A Tamil Nadu-based fertilizer company whose profitability improved after gas supplies, clearly visible in the last couple of quarters' results. After the recent rally it corrected to an attractive position. Buy the stock (around 80) with a strict stop loss around 67-68.

recommendations.note: Best of times likely over; wait, don't rush to buy
recommendations.detail: He thinks the best of times may be over. The promoters' copyright dispute and lack of market confidence have kept it forming lower tops and lower bottoms, and it is near 52-week lows. He would wait rather than rush to buy now.

recommendations.note: Long-term; slowly accumulate; good smaller-defence bet
recommendations.detail: For the long term he would certainly take it and slowly accumulate. It is one of the good bets among the smaller defence-pack stocks.

recommendations.note: Risky buy after recent fall/spike and correction
recommendations.detail: After the recent fall a spike came and the stock corrected again; it rose from the first week of October and is now around 500. It can be taken with risk, but the stop loss should be around 460-470.

recommendations.note: NBFC strength; medium-term view, no big short-term gains
recommendations.detail: NBFCs are performing better than banks, and in Poonawalla the promoters are inducting funds. It is at an ex-QIP rate around 450. Buy only with a medium-term view; do not expect big short-term gains.

recommendations.note: In-trend correction; earlier 650-670 unrealistic; sell on bounce
recommendations.detail: It is correcting in-trend with the market, not weakening in isolation. The earlier 650-670 was an unrealistic price, and with results not in line it keeps forming lower tops and lower bottoms, falling from around 450-460 to about 323. Wait for the Q3 result and sell into a rally closer to 450.

recommendations.note: Don't average; hold (asked with Cochin Shipyard)
recommendations.detail: Asked together with Cochin Shipyard (caller holds NSDL at 1,771). He advised not to average and to hold.

recommendations.note: Fundamentally sound but bought high; don't average
recommendations.detail: A fundamentally sound defence stock, but the caller bought very high near the 52-week high (2,464). Don't average now; do fresh buying only after the result, maybe closer to about 1,400 where it would trade at an attractive ~45 PE. Until then, hold.

recommendations.note: JSW Group link supports it; hold, exit after Q3 if no improvement
recommendations.detail: The link to the JSW Group keeps the stock supported despite being a penny stock (face value 23 rupees). Cement has a better future, but unless company performance improves, maybe exit after Q3; until then hold.

recommendations.note: Sell for listing profits
recommendations.detail: He advised selling for listing profits.
